note: state.block is typed Record<string,string> (from S.Record(S.String, S.String)); spread-merge with a dynamic key is a string-keyed record set -> R.set(state.block, key, value).

nativeSnippet: value === undefined ? {} : ({ [key]: value } as Record<K, V>)
note: `maybe` builds a Partial<Record<K,V>> with a typed key K extends string; R.singleton(key, value) returns Record<string,V> and would widen the typed key, and the empty-branch returns {}. Decide whether the typed-key contract matters before substituting.

Receiver `state` is a ProgressState S.Class instance; spreading it produces a plain object cast back to ProgressState. SPEC §5 forbids schema authoring; Struct.assign on a class instance is a behavior-adjacent change. Confirm a Struct merge preserves the class shape before substituting, or leave the struct spread as-is and only migrate the inner string-keyed `block` record.
Outer spread merges into a ProgressState S.Class instance; same class-instance caveat as line 468. The inner `block` record spread is the clear win (see line 479).
`current` is a ProgressState S.Class instance read from Ref; struct spread to a plain object. Same class-instance caveat as line 468.

`request` is an ExtractFramesRequest S.Class instance spread into a new constructor call to override two fields. This is idiomatic constructor-override; substituting Struct.assign offers little gain and risks the class-shape caveat. Likely leave as-is.
